component.name: SSHVault
component.summary: Zero-knowledge encrypted SSH and terminal client

description.p: SSHVault is a secure SSH client with zero-knowledge encrypted sync across devices. Manage SSH connections, keys, and snippets with end-to-end encryption — the server never sees your data.

ul.li: SSH terminal with full xterm emulation
Zero-knowledge sync with AES-256-GCM and Argon2id

description.p: Linux desktop integration pass: ssh:// + sftp:// URL handlers, system tray (AppIndicator/SNI) with favorites and active sessions, DBus service de.kiefer_networks.SSHVault for single-instance + external triggers from rofi/polybar/KRunner, KRunner plugin, libsecret-backed master-key storage in GNOME Keyring / KWallet, ssh-agent integration so git/scp/ansible/vscode-remote-ssh can use SSHVault keys, follow GNOME / KDE preferred color scheme via xdg-desktop-portal, and drag-and-drop SSH keys onto the window to auto-import.
Android: liboqs.so is now built with 16 KB ELF page alignment so Google Play accepts the upload on Android 15+ devices. Other native libs (libflutter, libapp, libsqlite3) were already aligned.

Drops READ_MEDIA_IMAGES / READ_MEDIA_VIDEO permission inherited from image_picker so Google Play accepts the upload (the app uses the Photo Picker, which needs no permission). Android disconnects now appear as a system-shade notification with an inline Reconnect action button; the prior in-app slide-up dialog is gone.

Android now uses the OS notification shade instead of in-app SnackBar overlays so messages no longer cover the UI. List padding above the FAB cluster bumped to 144 dp so the floating add/search buttons no longer sit on top of the last server tile's edit / info actions.

Fixes hybrid post-quantum SSH KEX wire format and shared-secret derivation against OpenSSH 9.9+ servers — mlkem768x25519 client public-key blob now sends the KEM half before the X25519 half, server reply parsing matches, and the resulting shared secret is HASH(kem_ss || x25519_ss) (SHA-256 for ML-KEM, SHA-512 for sntrup761) before being string-encoded into the exchange hash. 1.10.2 negotiated the algorithm but failed at the first encrypted packet.
CI fix: Flatpak liboqs module now uses CMAKE_INSTALL_LIBDIR instead of the meson-style --libdir flag (cmake-ninja rejected the latter).

Fixes liboqs Flatpak SHA256 mismatch and reduces the per-platform liboqs build to just ML-KEM-768 and sntrup761 so the macOS, Windows, Android and Flatpak release jobs finish without exhausting the runner's process table.

Adds hybrid post-quantum SSH key exchange (mlkem768x25519-sha256, sntrup761x25519-sha512@openssh.com) via the Open Quantum Safe liboqs library. Connects to OpenSSH 9.9+ servers configured for PQ-only KEX without breaking classical fallback.
Format pass fixes CI; no behavior changes from 1.9.2.
Connect dialog now explains which SSH algorithm class (KEX, cipher, MAC, host key) the server requires when none overlap with the client. README documents supported algorithms.
Fixes a widget test regression by routing native desktop notifications through defaultTargetPlatform; ships regenerated localization bindings.
Sync now propagates deletes via tombstones and uses per-entity last-write-wins for renames and reorders, fixing data resurrecting between devices.
System notifications on Linux and Windows; redesigned folder section headers; grid view honors mode toggle; search moved to FAB; expanded icon library; min/max/close window controls on Linux.
